title: Unknown-variant tests: missing combined deserializeâ†’serialize round-trip
description: test_unknown_tuple_variants_preserve_original_string (line 1844) only calls serde_json::to_string on a manually-constructed Unknown("..."). It never touches the deserializer. The deserialization side for unknowns is covered by separate tests (test_chat_kind_unknown_wire_value_becomes_unknown, etc.) but those only assert the resulting enum variant â€” they do not re-serialize. The two halves are never chained in a single test. PushUrgency has exactly this combined test (test_push_urgency_unknown_preserves_original_string): it does from_str then to_string in sequence, which is the only test that would catch a bug where deserialize captures the string but as_str() accidentally returns a hardcoded fallback. The three new enums should each have an equivalent combined round-trip test, or the existing test should be extended to chain both directions.
close_reason: Fixed: combined round-trip tests added in last commit
